compiled_state.title: As a developer or agent, ticket transition rejects deleted as a target status and treats deleted tickets as terminal when evaluating children
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Story

Prevent any path from transitioning a ticket to 'deleted' except through `ticket delete`, and ensure all subsystems that check closed/terminal status treat 'deleted' equivalently to 'closed'.

## Done Definitions

- [ ] `ticket transition <id> <current> deleted` exits non-zero with an explicit error message: 'deleted is not a valid transition target — use ticket delete <id> to delete a ticket'
- [ ] Status validation in ticket-transition.sh explicitly lists 'deleted' in a blocked-targets guard before the generic validation, so the error is surfaced clearly and not confused with 'invalid status'
- [ ] CLOSED_STATUSES in ticket-transition.sh Python inline block includes 'deleted' — parent closure gate no longer blocks when all remaining non-closed children are deleted
- [ ] CLOSED_STATUSES in ticket-unblock.py includes 'deleted' — dependency resolution treats deleted tickets as non-blocking
- [ ] sprint-next-batch.sh terminal status check includes 'deleted' — sprint batch selection never surfaces deleted tickets as candidates
- [ ] Unit tests: (a) transition to 'deleted' returns error with instructional message, (b) CLOSED_STATUSES recognizes 'deleted' in all three locations, (c) a parent with one deleted and one closed child passes the closure gate

## Considerations

- [Scope] Three independent CLOSED_STATUSES definitions exist (ticket-transition.sh Python inline, ticket-unblock.py, sprint-next-batch.sh) — all three must be updated in a single story to avoid divergence windows where one subsystem blocks parent closure while another allows it
- [Maintainability] Consider whether a shared constant or config key for CLOSED_STATUSES would prevent future divergence; if added, note it in ticket-cli-reference.md so the next developer knows where to look
- [Testing] The blocked-transition test must confirm the exact error message text matches what the instructional error in ticket-delete.sh tells the agent to expect, so discovery path is coherent
